Event: Navigates castaway group dynamics despite language barrier
Explanation: Adapting quickly to social dynamics helps his own survival and cohesion of the group.

Event: Is a brute / enforcer for his father-in-law
Explanation: He gains status and favor with Mr. Paik while violently harming others.

Event: Drowns in the submarine with Sun (chooses not to escape)
Explanation: His choice kills him but emotionally supports Sun by not leaving her to die alone.

Event: Defies Pryce by glaring and telling him to go to hell
Explanation: He preserves his dignity with no clear added harm or benefit to others in the scene.

Event: Protects Sun determinedly in various moments
Explanation: His persistent efforts keep Sun safe and strengthen their bond.

Event: Insists Locke not bring Sun or their baby back to the Island
Explanation: He sacrifices future reunion to keep them away from danger as he understands it.

Event: Gives Locke his wedding ring and tells him to say Jin is dead
Explanation: He gives up a precious token and risks Sun’s grief to keep her safe from the Island.

Event: Lacks trust in others, driving a wedge between him and Sun
Explanation: His distrust damages his marriage and offers no benefit to him or anyone else.

Event: Moves past his lack of trust
Explanation: Overcoming distrust greatly improves his relationship and his own well-being.

Event: Beats up Michael over Mr. Paik’s watch
Explanation: He defends his and Paik’s honor at the cost of violently harming Michael and group relations.

Event: Destroys Sun’s garden
Explanation: He lashes out and wrecks her peaceful project, hurting her with no gain to himself.

Event: Helps Sun replant her garden later
Explanation: Repairing the damage improves their relationship and restores something valuable to her.

Event: Learns English and becomes fluent over time
Explanation: Language acquisition greatly benefits him and improves coordination with the group.

Event: Survives destruction of the freighter and days adrift at sea
Explanation: His resilience preserves his life and allows him to return to help others later.

Event: Jin’s mother dumps him on his father
Explanation: She abandons him, likely worsening her own prospects while deeply harming her child.

Event: Jin’s father raises him as his own despite paternity doubts
Explanation: He gains emotional fulfillment and gives Jin care and upbringing.

Event: Has been an oppressive, angry husband to Sun
Explanation: His behavior harms Sun and undermines his own marriage without real benefit.

Event: Becomes a better, more faithful husband over time
Explanation: His growth strengthens mutual loyalty and long-term happiness.

Event: Becomes good friends with Michael after early conflict
Explanation: Forging friendship improves both their support networks and cooperation.

Event: Is genuinely happy to see Michael again and shares Sun’s pregnancy news
Explanation: His warmth and sharing rebuild trust and give Michael meaningful hope.

Event: Has an epic freak-out over losing golf and past humiliations
Explanation: The outburst isolates him further and distresses others with no benefit.

Event: Stays on the submarine to die with his trapped wife
Explanation: He sacrifices his own life to provide Sun emotional comfort in her final moments.

Event: Viciously beats men to a pulp for insulting/cheating father-in-law
Explanation: He protects his position and mitigates harsher retribution by brutalizing others.

Event: Does this in part to prevent Mr. Paik from killing them
Explanation: Compared to likely execution, his violence, while severe, arguably leaves them alive.

Event: Forgives Sun’s extramarital affair almost instantly
Explanation: Quick forgiveness heals their relationship and relieves Sun’s guilt.
